stem: A clinical study study measures the diastolic blood pressure (mmHg) for a sample of 25 patients. The sample mean is 199.4 mmHg with a sample standard deviation of 38.5 mmHg. However, 3 of the 25 observations appear to be outliers (values more than 2.5 standard deviations from the mean). If these outliers are removed, the remaining 22 observations have a mean of 188.6 mmHg and standard deviation of 26.6 mmHg. Estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval that accounts for the uncertainty about whether outliers should be included.

ground_truth.answer: With all data: mean = 199.4, 95% CI = [183.5, 215.3]. Without outliers: mean = 188.6, 95% CI = [177.3, 199.9]. Combined interval accounting for analytic uncertainty: [177.3, 215.3]. Point estimate: 194.0 mmHg.

solution_steps: The outlier treatment choice creates structural uncertainty. Combined interval: [177.3, 215.3].
common_failure_modes: Using only the full sample without considering outlier impact
Removing outliers without reporting both analyses
Not recognizing that the choice of analysis method is itself a source of uncertainty
